_**User**_

Prompt 2 — Diagnóstico del error de Supabase
Este lo usás en el chat nuevo de Cursor. Guardalo en ia/entrega-1/prompts/bloque-1/cursor-04-diagnostico-supabase-url.md:


Diagnóstico — Error "Invalid path specified in request URL"
Contexto
La app Juntadas está desarrollada con React Native + Expo SDK 55 + TypeScript. El backend es Supabase. Al intentar registrarse, iniciar sesión o enviar instrucciones de recuperación de contraseña, aparece el error: "Invalid path specified in request URL".
El error se muestra en pantalla como un banner rojo, lo que indica que está siendo capturado correctamente por el manejo de errores del servicio. No es un crash.
El archivo .env tiene:
EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://[proyecto].supabase.co
EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...
Lo que necesito
Analizá los siguientes archivos sin modificar nada todavía:

mobile/src/lib/supabase/client.ts
mobile/src/config/env.ts
mobile/src/features/auth/services/authService.ts
mobile/src/features/auth/hooks/useAuth.ts

Para cada archivo respondé:

¿Cómo se lee y usa la URL de Supabase?
¿Hay algún lugar donde se modifique, concatene o transforme la URL?
¿Hay algo que pueda estar causando que llegue una URL malformada al cliente de Supabase?

Al finalizar el análisis:

Identificá la causa raíz del error
Describí el fix exacto con el código corregido
Listá todos los archivos que hay que modificar
No apliques ningún cambio todavía — esperá confirmación

Restricciones

No modificar ningún archivo
No hacer commits
No instalar dependencias

---

I need to check the app configuration and Expo settings for environment variable loading, as well as verify if any other parts of the codebase modify URLs. The error message "Invalid path specified in request URL" indicates a Supabase/postgrest issue likely caused by trailing slashes, malformed paths, or undefined environment variables in the Supabase URL configuration. Common issues include trailing slashes in EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, missing runtime environment variables, or placeholder URLs remaining in .env files despite appearing correct.</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

Reviso cómo Expo carga las variables de entorno y busco otros usos de la URL de Supabase.

Supabase Storage requires file paths to be correctly structured to ensure files are stored and retrieved accurately.\n[...]\nUsing unsupported characters in the file path. Incorrectly structured file paths, such as missing slashes or incorrect directory names. Exceeding the maximum allowed length for a file path.\n[...]\nTo resolve the InvalidFilePath error, follow these steps:\n[...]\n### Step 1: Verify the File Path Format\n[...]\nEnsure that the file path follows the correct format. A typical file path should look like this:\n[...]\nbucket_name/folder_name/file_name.extension\n[...]\nMake sure to replace bucket_name, folder_name, and file_name.extension with your actual bucket, folder, and file names.\n[...]\n### Step 2: Check for Unsupported Characters\n[...]\nEnsure that your file path does not contain any unsupported characters. Avoid using special characters like \\, :, *, ?, \", \u003c, \u003e, and |.\n[...]\n### Step 3: Validate Path Length\n[...]\nEnsure that your file path does not exceed the maximum length allowed by Supabase Storage. In TestFlight, one common fix is to add an iOS ATS exception for your Supabase domain (supabase.co) in app’s Info.plist (including NSExceptionAllowsInsecureHTTPLoads and NSIncludesSubdomains). Also ensure your Supabase Auth “Site URL” and any redirectTo URL match exactly what’s in Supabase’s Redirect URLs settings. [1][2][3]\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eSupabase Connection Fails on TestFlight, Works Locally (Expo Managed Workflow)\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://stackoverflow.com/questions/79578943/supabase-connection-fails-on-testflight-works-locally-expo-managed-workflow\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eI'm running into an issue where my app connects perfectly to Supabase when running locally (via expo start or in Expo Go), but fails to connect when I test the app through TestFlight. According to Sentry it's an AuthRetryableFetchError.\n[...]\nconst supabaseUrl = process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL as string;\nconst supabaseAnonKey = process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY as string;\n[...]\n// Add error checking for missing environment variables\nif (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseAnonKey) {\n  console.warn(\n    \"Missing Supabase environment variables. Some features may not work.\"\n  );\n}\n[...]\nexport const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey, {\n  auth: {\n    storage: AsyncStorage,\n    autoRefreshToken: true,\n    persistSession: true,\n    detectSessionInUrl: false,\n  },\n});\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n- Verified that the Supabase URL and anon key are correctly included in the EAS build (via eas.json / app.config.js).\n- I added to the app.json.\n- I added Sentry to have more details on the error.\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\"infoPlist\": {\n        \"NSAppTransportSecurity\": {\n          \"NSAllowsArbitraryLoads\": true\n        }\n      }\n[...]\nIs there a\n[...]\nI'm having a similar issue with connecting to my supabase project using the IOS simulator.\n[...]\nI wonder if it is because Apple ATS sees a URL that ends in \"supabase.co\" and doesn't like it.\n[...]\nIf I update my infoPlist like this, it works. I don't know if this is a good long term solution though.\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\"ios\": {\n      \"supportsTablet\": true,\n      \"bundleIdentifier\": \"com.xxxxxxx.expoapp\",\n      \"infoPlist\": {\n        \"ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption\": false,\n        \"NSAppTransportSecurity\": {\n          \"NSAllowsArbitraryLoads\": true,\n          \"NSExceptionDomains\": {\n            \"supabase.co\": {\n              \"NSExceptionAllowsInsecureHTTPLoads\": true,\n              \"NSIncludesSubdomains\": true\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eredirect_uri_mismatch and requested path is invalid errors despite correct configuration\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://stackoverflow.com/questions/79761921/redirect-uri-mismatch-and-requested-path-is-invalid-errors-despite-correct-confi\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e&#96;&#96;&#96;\n    URL Configuration:\n\n        Site URL: https://\u003cmy-supabase-project-ref\u003e.supabase.co\n\n        Redirect URLs:\n\n            https://auth.expo.io/@\u003cmy-expo-username\u003e/\u003cmy-project-slug\u003e/**\n\n            \u003cmy-app-scheme\u003e://**\n\n            exp://**\n\n    **Google Provider:** Enabled with the correct Web Client ID and Client Secret from Google Cloud.\n[...]\n**Approach 1: Using Supabase's signInWithOAuth (The \"requested path is invalid\" error)**\n[...]\nmakeRedirectUri({\n[...]\nReturns https://auth.expo.io/@\u003cmy-expo\n[...]\nslug\u003e\n  \n  const { data\n[...]\nerror } = await\n[...]\nWithOAuth({\n    provider:\n[...]\noptions: {\n      redirectTo: redirectUrl\n[...]\n,\n    },\n[...]\n?.url)\n[...]\n(data.\n[...]\n, redirectUrl);\n  }\n};\n[...]\n**Result:** After selecting a Google account, the browser redirects to https://.supabase.co/#access\\_token=... and displays a JSON error: {\"error\":\"requested path is invalid\"}. It never redirects back to the Expo Go proxy URL.\n[...]\n**Approach 2: Using expo-auth-session/providers/google directly (The redirect\\_uri\\_mismatch error)**\n[...]\n**Result:** When the Google login screen opens, it immediately shows an Error 400: redirect\\_uri\\_mismatch. The request details show that expo-auth-session is sending redirect\\_uri=exp://192.168.X.X:8081 to Google, which is not a valid HTTP/HTTPS URI and cannot be added to the Google Cloud Console allowlist.\n[...]\n**Approach 1 fails** because Supabase doesn't seem to correctly handle the redirectTo parameter when it's an Expo proxy URL, defaulting to its Site URL and causing an \"invalid path\" error.\n[...]\n**Approach 2 fails** because expo-auth-session sends a non-HTTP deep link (exp://...) as the redirect\\_uri to Google, which Google rejects.\n[...]\nIs it possible to implement Supabase Google OAuth on Expo SDK 53 with Expo Go? Is there a specific configuration for the redirectUri or a different approach that I'm missing to make this work without having to do a full development build for every test?\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eRedirect URLs | Supabase Docs\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/redirect-urls\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eWhen using passwordless sign-ins or third-party providers, the Supabase client library provides a &#96;redirectTo&#96; parameter to specify where to redirect the user after authentication. The URL in &#96;redirectTo&#96; should match the Redirect URLs list configuration.\n[...]\nTo configure allowed redirect URLs, go to the URL Configuration page. Once you've added necessary URLs, you can use the URL you want the user to be redirected to in the &#96;redirectTo&#96; parameter.\n[...]\nThe Site URL in URL Configuration defines the default redirect URL when no &#96;redirectTo&#96; is specified in the code. Change this from &#96;http://localhost:3000&#96; to your production URL (e.g., https://example.com). This setting is critical for email confirmations and password resets.\n[...]\nWhile the \"globstar\" (&#96;**&#96;) is useful for local development and preview URLs, we recommend setting the exact redirect URL path for your site URL in production.\n[...]\nVercel provides an environment variable for the URL of the deployment called &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_VERCEL_URL&#96;. See the Vercel docs for more details. You can use this variable to dynamically redirect depending on the environment. You should also set the value of the environment variable called NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL, this should be set to your site URL in production environment to ensure that redirects function correctly.\n[...]\nlet url =\n    process?.env?.NEXT\n[...]\nprocess?.env?.NEXT_\n[...]\n// Automatically set by Vercel\n[...]\n'\n[...]\n0/'\n  // Make sure to include\n[...]\n://&#96; when not localhost.\n  url = url.\n[...]\n('http') ? url : &#96;\n[...]\nMake sure to include a\n[...]\n&#96;.\n[...]\n= url.\n[...]\nconst { data, error } = await supabase.auth\n[...]\nOAuth({\n  provider:\n[...]\ngithub',\n  options: {\n[...]\n(),\n  },\n})\n[...]\nWhen authentication fails, the user will still be redirected to the redirect URL provided. However, the error details will be returned as query fragments in the URL. You can parse these query fragments and show a custom error message to the user. For example:\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eExpo Dev Client (iOS) + Supabase — TypeError: Network request failed in custom build (&#96;expo run&#96;)\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://stackoverflow.com/questions/79587695/expo-dev-client-ios-supabase-typeerror-network-request-failed-in-custom-b\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAfter switching, all Supabase network requests started failing on iOS simulator with this error:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n(NOBRIDGE) ERROR  Error fetching initial session: Network request failed undefined  \n(NOBRIDGE) ERROR  [TypeError: Network request failed]  \n(NOBRIDGE) ERROR  [AuthRetryableFetchError: Network request failed]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\nEven when I switch the dev server host in the Expo Dev menu to &#96;http://localhost:8081&#96; or my LAN IP (&#96;http://192.168.0.166:8081&#96;), the error still occurs. Everything worked fine before in Expo Go.\n[...]\n- ✅ The project is running locally at &#96;http://192.168.0.166:8081&#96;\n- ✅ This URL in safari in the iOS Simulator does not throw this error and loads data (as evident in console logs but does not have any UI just the loading screen)\n- ✅ It worked in **Expo Go** before I installed the new dependencies\n- ❌ It fails in the **dev client** built with &#96;expo run:ios&#96;\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { AppState } from 'react-native';\nimport 'react-native-url-polyfill/auto';\nimport * as SecureStore from 'expo-secure-store';\nimport { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';\n[...]\nconst supabaseUrl = process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL || '';\nconst supabaseAnonKey = process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY || '';\n[...]\nexport const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey, {\n  auth: {\n    storage: SecureStoreStorage,\n    autoRefreshToken: true,\n    persistSession: true,\n    detectSessionInUrl: false,\n  },\n});\n[...]\nGot this error after it working once:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nFailed to load app from http://192.0.0.2:8081 with\nerror: The resource could not be loaded because\nthe App Transport Security policy requires the use\nof a secure connection.\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n- **user30345391**: New Error: TypeError: Network request failed at anonymous (192.168.0.166:8081/index.bundle//…)\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eReact Native - Expo, Password Reset, redirect URL and Exchange authorization code. · Issue #843 · supabase/supabase-js\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/issues/843\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e- Sometimes it's redirect to base URL &#96;exp://127.0.0.1:8081&#96; and some time it is redirecting to provided URL &#96;exp://127.0.0.1:8081/--/resetPassword&#96;,\n\n- Then at final approach as supabase docs:\n[...]\n.\nconst code =\n[...]\n.searchParams.\n[...]\nquery parameter looks like this:\n[...]\n&#96;exp://127.0.0.1:8081/--/resetPassword#access_token=123\u0026expires_in=3600\u0026refresh_token=123\u0026token_type=bearer\u0026type=recovery\"&#96;\n[...]\n\u003e To take advantage of the exchange code route method, you need to set auth \u003e flowType option to pkce for your supabase client\n[...]\n\u003e \u003e However, the query parameter looks like this:\n\u003e \n\u003e exp://127.0.0.1:8081/--/resetPassword#access_token=123\u0026expires_in=3600\u0026refresh_token=123\u0026token_type=bearer\u0026type=recovery\"\n\u003e \n\u003e Multiple problems here:\n\u003e \n\u003e 1. As @j4w8n mentioned you need flowtype set to pkce.\n\u003e 2. For non PKCE flow you need to replace the # with ? to be able to extract it from the query.\n\u003e \n\u003e Augment this for PKCE flow once you have that enabled. You also need URL polyfill to be installed.\n\u003e \n\u003e &#96;&#96;&#96;ts\n\u003e const parsedUrl = url_with_code_as_hashtag.replace(\"#\", \"?\");\n\u003e const url = new URL(parsedUrl);\n\u003e const access_token = url.searchParams.get(\"access_token\");\n\u003e const refresh_token = url.searchParams.get(\"refresh_token\");\n\u003e \n\u003e // Handle code exchanges\n\u003e &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003e \n\u003e I'm using Supabase without PKCE with success from the above code.\n[...]\nHowever, the query parameter\n[...]\n0.1\n[...]\n808\n[...]\nPassword#access_\n[...]\n=123\u0026expires_in=3600\u0026\n[...]\n23\u0026token_type=\n[...]\nyou need to replace\n[...]\nfor PKCE flow once\n[...]\n\u003e \u003e &#96;&#96;&#96;ts\n\u003e \u003e const parsedUrl = url_with_code_as_hashtag.replace(\"#\n[...]\n\"?\");\n\u003e \u003e const url = new URL(parsedUrl);\n[...]\n\u003e const access_token = url.searchParams.get\n[...]\n_token = url.searchParams\n[...]\nget(\"refresh_token\");\n[...]\n\u003e \u003e // Handle code exchanges\n[...]\nI'm using Supabase\n[...]\nfrom the above code.\n[...]\n\u003e That is by design. The supabase client, in non-pkce mode, should be handling that scenario automatically.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

[internal/api/errors.go at f3425cf7 · supabase/auth](https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/f3425cf7/internal/api/errors.go)\n\nSynthesis:\nThis usually means your signup request URL is malformed: include the full scheme. Call https://\u003cproject-ref\u003e.supabase.co/auth/v1/signup (with POST), not \u003cproject-ref\u003e.supabase.co/auth/v1/signup. In one Supabase issue, the docs/curl examples used the version without https:// and returned 405; switching to the full URL fixed it [1].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eUnable to Use CURL APIs \"405 Method Not Allowed\" · Issue #30104 · supabase/supabase\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/supabase/supabase/issues/30104\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e## Unable to Use CURL APIs \"405 Method Not Allowed\"\n[...]\nI go to my projects API docs and copy the CURL request with my public anon key.\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ncurl -X POST '[retracted].supabase.co/auth/v1/signup' \\\n-H \"apikey: [retracted but public api key]\" \\\n-H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n-d '{\n  \"email\": \"someone@email.com\",\n  \"password\": \"kZPMcgVvHOfzUQMuqDOD\"\n}'\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\nI replicate to Postman and Bruno and get the same error. \"405 Method Not Allowed\"\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003c 405 Method Not Allowed\n\u003c date: Fri, 25 Oct 2024 16:51:58 GMT\n\u003c content-length: 0\n\u003c connection: keep-alive\n\u003c cf-ray: 8d83b6df88c21f46-DEN\n\u003c cf-cache-status: DYNAMIC\n\u003c allow: POST\n\u003c strict-transport-security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains\n\u003c vary: Origin, Accept-Encoding\n\u003c sb-gateway-version: 1\n\u003c x-envoy-attempt-count: 1\n\u003c x-envoy-upstream-service-time: 0\n\u003c server: cloudflare\n\u003c alt-svc: h3=\":443\"; ma=86400\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n\u003e All of these issues were resolved when I switched both the API client and the CURL statement to use\n\u003e \n\u003e &#96;https://[retracted].supabase.co/auth/v1/signup&#96;\n\u003e \n\u003e instead of\n\u003e \n\u003e &#96;[retracted].supabase.co/auth/v1/signup&#96;\n\u003e \n\u003e Can probably close this ticket but I am curious if the API Docs should have this? Thank you to @silentworks and @GaryAustin1 for their help.\n[...]\n\u003e You should modify your issue to mention dashboard and NOT close it. It appears the API docs are not correct as without that I confirm I get a cloudflare error.\n[...]\n\u003e Hi all - Where in the dashboard are you seeing the incorrect URL?\n[...]\n\u003e Looks like this had a PR done: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/pull/30136\n[...]\n\u003e And I confirm fixed in my dashboard.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eBug report: Dashboard + signup API failing with 500 – invalid sb_temp_ key · Issue #44945 · supabase/supabase\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/supabase/supabase/issues/44945\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e1. Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → Users → Add user → \"Failed to create user: Database error creating new user\"\n2. Application signup endpoint &#96;/auth/v1/signup&#96; returns 500\n[...]\nExisting users can log in normally via &#96;/auth/v1/token&#96; — only user creation is broken.\n[...]\n1. Call &#96;/auth/v1/signup&#96; with email + password\n2. Returns 500\n[...]\nBoth endpoints show invalid temporary key in logs:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"error\": \"invalid\",\n  \"prefix\": \"sb_temp_tw\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\ndifferent &#96;sb_temp_&#96; prefixes\n[...]\n&#96;sb_temp_DT&#96;, &#96;sb_temp_JV&#96;, &#96;sb_temp_DO&#96;, &#96;sb_temp\n[...]\nNew users can be created via dashboard and via &#96;/auth/v1/signup&#96;.\n[...]\n\u003e Yes, I have a trigger on auth.users that creates a profile record on signup:\n\u003e \n\u003e CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created\n\u003e AFTER INSERT ON auth.users\n\u003e FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION handle_new_user();\n\u003e\n[...]\n\u003e The function inserts into a public.profiles table with SECURITY DEFIN\n[...]\n\u003e \"method\": \"POST\",\n\u003e \"path\": \"/auth/v1/admin/users\",\n[...]\n\u003e \"port\": null,\n\u003e \"protocol\": \"https:\",\n[...]\n\u003e \"sb\": [\n\u003e {\n\u003e \"apikey\": [\n\u003e {\n\u003e \"apikey\": [\n\u003e {\n\u003e \"error\": \"invalid\",\n\u003e \"hash\": null,\n\u003e \"prefix\": \"sb_temp_ds\"\n\u003e }\n\u003e ],\n[...]\n\u003e \"authorization\": [\n[...]\n\u003e \"error\": \"invalid\",\n\u003e \"hash\": null,\n\u003e \"prefix\": \"sb_temp_ds\"\n[...]\n\u003e \"url\": \"https://rvriovqnyoojdyinujel.supabase.co/auth/v1/admin\n[...]\n\u003e It looks like an issue with your project with temp key being rejected.\n\u003e \n\u003e You'd have to reach out to support on this one - support@supabase.com, which plan are you on are? Free or pro?\n[...]\nThis helped me and I have fix it.\n[...]\n\u003e \n\u003e [\n[...]\n\u003e \"detail\": null,\n[...]\n\u003e \"error_severity\": \"ERROR\",\n[...]\n\u003e \"event_\n[...]\n\": \"current transaction is aborted, commands ignored until\n[...]\n-7a8d65\n[...]\n\u003e {\n\u003e \"detail\": null,\n\u003e \"error_severity\": \"ERROR\",\n\u003e \"event_message\": \"relation \\\"profiles\\\" does not exist\",\n\u003e \"hint\": null,\n\u003e \"id\": \"d1d55ef6-f25b-4a92-aaba-e5d4b9fba6f2\",\n\u003e \"identifier\": \"rvriovqnyoojdyinujel\",\n\u003e \"timestamp\": 17\n[...]\n6372200640000\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eRegistration throws multiple different errors in irregular intervals · Issue #19333 · supabase/supabase\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/supabase/supabase/issues/19333\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e'\n\nexport async function POST(request: Request) {\n  const requestUrl = new URL(request.url)\n  const formData = await request.formData()\n  const email = String(formData.get('email'))\n  const password = String(formData.get('password'))\n  const supabase = createRouteHandlerClient({ cookies })\n\n  const { error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({\n    email,\n    password,\n    options: {\n      emailRedirectTo: &#96;${requestUrl.origin}/auth/callback&#96;,\n    },\n  })\n\n  if (error) {\n    return NextResponse.redirect(\n      &#96;${requestUrl.origin}/register?error=auth-failed&#96;,\n      {\n        // a 301 status is required to redirect from a POST to a GET route\n        status: 301,\n      }\n    )\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.redirect(&#96;${requestUrl.origin}/register/confirm&#96;, {\n    // a 301 status is required to redirect from a POST to a GET route\n    status: 301,\n  })\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n-nextjs\n[...]\nexport async function GET(request: Request) {\n  // The &#96;/auth/callback&#96; route is required for the server-side auth flow implemented\n  // by the Auth Helpers package. It exchanges an auth code for the user's session.\n  // https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/auth-helpers/nextjs#managing-sign-in-with-code-exchange\n  const requestUrl = new URL(request.url)\n  const code = requestUrl.searchParams.get('code')\n\n  if (code) {\n    const supabase = createRouteHandlerClient({ cookies })\n    const { error } = await supabase.auth.exchangeCodeForSession(code)\n    if (error) throw error\n  }\n\n  const next = requestUrl.searchParams.get('next')\n\n  // URL to redirect to after sign in process completes\n  return NextResponse.redirect(\n    next ? &#96;${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASEURL || ''}${next}&#96; : requestUrl.origin\n  )\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n\u003e @ramsaysewell The issue is still open, I just posted it in the wrong repo, see the very first line of the description. Here's the open issue.\n\u003e \n\u003e However, I managed to get rid of the errors by upgrading to the &#96;ssr&#96; package and pure use server side authentication. Hope that helps!\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003esupabase.auth.sign_up failing recently (used to work perfectly) · Issue #1796 · supabase/auth\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/supabase/auth/issues/1796\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Issue: supabase/auth #1796\n\n- Repository: supabase/auth | A JWT based API for managing users and issuing JWT tokens | 2K stars | Go\n\n## supabase.auth.sign_up failing recently (used to work perfectly)\n\n- Author: @thierrymoudiki\n- State: open\n- Labels: bug\n- Created: 2024-10-06T19:13:42Z\n- Updated: 2024-10-11T01:00:17Z\n\n# Bug report\n\n- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application.\n- [x] I confirm I have searched the Docs, GitHub Discussions, and Discord.\n\n## Describe the bug\n\n&#96;{\"detail\":\"Email address \\\"firstname.lastname@pm.me\\\" cannot be used as it is not authorized\"}&#96;\n\n## To Reproduce\n\nTry to sign-up to https://www.techtonique.net/\n\nLogging-in works as it used to. <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The error likely originates from either the Kong gateway or the Supabase API gateway.

The error likely stems from the environment variable `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` containing an incorrect value such as a placeholder, missing protocol, trailing path segments, or whitespace. <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Important insight from SupabaseClient constructor:

If baseUrl is `https://project.supabase.co/`, the authUrl correctly becomes `https://project.supabase.co/auth/v1`. However, if baseUrl is `https://project.supabase.co/rest/v1/` (incorrectly including the full API path), the resulting authUrl becomes `https://project.supabase.co/rest/v1/auth/v1`, which is invalid.

Additionally, when the URL uses `[proyecto]` in the hostname, it may be interpreted as IPv6 bracket notation, potentially causing parsing issues or malformed URLs.</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

Verifico si el endpoint de Auth responde correctamente con la URL del `.env`.

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

## Diagnóstico — "Invalid path specified in request URL"

Prompt guardado en `juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/prompts/bloque-1/cursor-04-diagnostico-supabase-url.md`.

Reproduje el error en runtime: ocurre cuando la URL base incluye un path extra como `/rest/v1`. El SDK construye `https://xxx.supabase.co/rest/v1/auth/v1/...`, que el gateway de Supabase rechaza con ese mensaje exacto.

---

### 1. `mobile/src/lib/supabase/client.ts`

**¿Cómo se lee y usa la URL?**
- Importa `env` desde `@/config/env`.
- Pasa `env.supabase.url` y `env.supabase.anonKey` directamente a `createClient()`.

**¿Se modifica, concatena o transforma?**
- No. Es un passthrough directo.

**¿Puede causar URL malformada?**
- No por sí mismo. Solo propaga lo que viene de `env.ts`. El SDK internamente arma sub-rutas (`auth/v1`, `rest/v1`, etc.) a partir de esa URL base.

---

### 2. `mobile/src/config/env.ts`

**¿Cómo se lee y usa la URL?**
- Lee `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` en build time (Metro/Expo la inyecta desde `.env`).

**¿Se modifica, concatena o transforma?**
- No. Se asigna tal cual, con non-null assertion (`!`).

**¿Puede causar URL malformada?**
- No transforma nada, pero tampoco valida ni normaliza. Si `.env` trae:
  - `/rest/v1` al final → error exacto en auth
  - placeholder `https://[proyecto].supabase.co` → crash al iniciar el cliente
  - URL sin `https://` → crash al iniciar el cliente
  - valor vacío/undefined → crash al iniciar el cliente

---

### 3. `mobile/src/features/auth/services/authService.ts`

**¿Cómo se lee y usa la URL?**
- Usa el singleton `supabase` importado de `@/lib/supabase/client`.
- Llama `supabase.auth.signUp`, `signInWithPassword`, `resetPasswordForEmail`, etc.

**¿Se modifica, concatena o transforma?**
- No. Solo pasa email/password; la URL la resuelve el cliente.

**¿Puede causar URL malformada?**
- No. El error se propaga vía `error.message` → `mapAuthError()` → banner rojo.

---

### 4. `mobile/src/features/auth/hooks/useAuth.ts`

**¿Cómo se lee y usa la URL?**
- No interactúa con la URL. Delega todo a `authService`.

**¿Se modifica, concatena o transforma?**
- No.

**¿Puede causar URL malformada?**
- No.

---

## Causa raíz

**La URL en `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` incluye un path de API (típicamente `/rest/v1`) en lugar de ser solo la URL base del proyecto.**

El SDK hace esto internamente:

```typescript
this.authUrl = new URL('auth/v1', baseUrl)
```

Si `baseUrl` es `https://xxx.supabase.co/rest/v1/`, el endpoint queda:

`https://xxx.supabase.co/rest/v1/auth/v1/token` → **"Invalid path specified in request URL"**

Lo confirmé con una prueba local usando `@supabase/supabase-js`:

| URL en `.env` | Resultado en `signInWithPassword` |
|---|---|
| `https://xxx.supabase.co` | ✅ Funciona (`Invalid login credentials`) |
| `https://xxx.supabase.co/rest/v1` | ❌ **Invalid path specified in request URL** |
| `https://[proyecto].supabase.co` | ❌ Crash al crear cliente |
| Sin `https://` | ❌ Crash al crear cliente |

**Nota:** Tu `.env` actual ya tiene la URL correcta (`https://tgxedyffizwbgidexpfj.supabase.co`, sin `/rest/v1`). Si el error persiste, probablemente Metro sigue usando un bundle cacheado con el valor anterior — hay que reiniciar con caché limpia.

---

## Fix exacto (sin aplicar)

### Fix principal — `.env`

```env
EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://tgxedyffizwbgidexpfj.supabase.co
EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...
```

Reglas:
- Solo la URL base: `https://<project-ref>.supabase.co`
- Sin `/rest/v1`, `/auth/v1`, `/storage/v1`
- Con `https://`
- Sin placeholders (`[proyecto]`)
- Sin espacios ni comillas

Después de corregir:

```bash
npx expo start --clear
```

### Fix opcional defensivo — `env.ts`

*(Las reglas del proyecto dicen no tocar `env.ts` sin decisión explícita; esto es opcional.)*

```typescript
/**
 * Normaliza la URL base de Supabase para evitar paths duplicados
 * cuando alguien copia la URL desde la pestaña API del dashboard.
 */
const normalizeSupabaseUrl = (rawUrl: string | undefined): string => {
  const trimmed = rawUrl?.trim() ?? '';

  if (!trimmed) {
    throw new Error('EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL no está definida. Revisá el archivo .env');
  }

  // Quita paths como /rest/v1 que rompen auth/storage al concatenarse
  const withoutApiPath = trimmed.replace(/\/(rest|auth|storage|functions)\/v1\/?$/i, '');
  const baseUrl = withoutApiPath.replace(/\/+$/, '');

  if (!/^https:\/\/[a-z0-9-]+\.supabase\.co$/i.test(baseUrl)) {
    throw new Error(
      'EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL inválida. Debe ser https://<project-ref>.supabase.co',
    );
  }

  return baseUrl;
};

export const env = {
  supabase: {
    url: normalizeSupabaseUrl(process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL),
    anonKey: process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!.trim(),
  },
} as const;
```

---

## Archivos a modificar

| Archivo | Obligatorio | Qué cambiar |
|---|---|---|
| `mobile/.env` | ✅ Sí | URL base sin paths extra |
| `mobile/.env.example` | Recomendado | Documentar formato correcto con comentario |
| `mobile/src/config/env.ts` | Opcional | Normalización/validación defensiva |

**No requieren cambios:** `client.ts`, `authService.ts`, `useAuth.ts`.

---

## Pendiente de validación

1. Confirmar que `.env` no tiene `/rest/v1` (el actual parece correcto).
2. Reiniciar Expo con `--clear` tras cualquier cambio en `.env`.
3. Probar login con credenciales incorrectas: debería mostrar *"Email o contraseña incorrectos"*, no el error de path.
4. Si persiste, revisar en Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → URL Configuration que **Site URL** no sea la URL del proyecto Supabase (debe ser la URL de tu app).

---

**Archivos creados:** `juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/prompts/bloque-1/cursor-04-diagnostico-supabase-url.md`

**Archivos analizados (sin modificar):** `client.ts`, `env.ts`, `authService.ts`, `useAuth.ts`, `.env`

¿Querés que aplique el fix en `.env` y/o la validación defensiva en `env.ts`?

---

_**User**_

Guardalo en ia/entrega-1/prompts/bloque-1/cursor-05-fixes-cierre-bloque-1.md y pegalo en Cursor Agent:


Fixes — Cierre del Bloque 1: Autenticación
Contexto
El Bloque 1 de autenticación está casi completo. Quedan dos fixes puntuales antes de cerrar el bloque.
Fix 1 — Traducir error de rate limit al español
En mobile/src/features/auth/services/authService.ts existe una función mapAuthError que traduce los errores de Supabase Auth al español. Falta mapear el error de rate limit que actualmente llega en inglés al usuario.
El mensaje original de Supabase es:
"For security purposes, you can only request this after X seconds"
Agregá este caso al mapAuthError para que detecte ese mensaje y lo reemplace por:
"Por seguridad, esperá unos segundos antes de intentarlo nuevamente."
El match debe ser flexible — usá includes('security purposes') o similar para capturar el mensaje independientemente del número de segundos que indique.
Fix 2 — Agregar CompleteProfileScreen a MainNavigator
En mobile/src/navigation/MainNavigator.tsx la pantalla CompleteProfileScreen figura como () => null placeholder. Importá la pantalla real desde @/features/auth/screens/CompleteProfileScreen y reemplazá el placeholder.
Fix 3 — Verificación general de AuthNavigator y MainNavigator
Revisá ambos navigators y confirmá que:

Todas las pantallas del flujo auth tienen su import real (no () => null)
CompleteProfileScreen está en MainNavigator y no en AuthNavigator
Las rutas usan siempre las constantes de Routes, nunca strings hardcodeados

Restricciones

No instalar dependencias nuevas
